summary: Aligned local Linux PR readiness with hosted Linux CI by making just ci-local run direct-capture-preflight before verify and coverage, then documenting that gate in the PR template and Linux/repo guidance.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: A local Linux CI or PR-readiness command includes `nix develop -c just direct-capture-preflight` before broader checks.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: `just ci-local` now invokes `direct-capture-preflight` before `verify` and `coverage`; commands.log records `nix develop -c just ci-local` running in that order.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: PR template and Linux/repo documentation identify the direct-capture preflight as part of Linux PR readiness.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: Updated .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md, README.md, docs/ENGINEERING.md, docs/LINUX_ORCHESTRATOR.md, and docs/DIRECT_CAPTURE_HELPER_PREFLIGHT.md.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Evidence records local `nix develop -c just direct-capture-preflight`, the updated local gate, and `nix develop -c just check`.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: evidence/m4-direct-capture-local-ci-parity/commands.log records all three commands with zero exit status.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: macOS validation remains native and is not made responsible for Linux/Nix direct-capture dependencies.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: PR template and docs keep macOS validation on `just macos-check` and state that Linux/Nix direct-capture dependency validation is not routed through macOS.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: No runtime capture, product QUIC, portal, capture-to-NVENC, zero-copy, cross-device, decode, render, presentation, or latency claim is made.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: Documentation and evidence notes describe this node as command ordering and guidance only.

realWorldValidation.evidence: This node changes local Linux verification command ordering and documentation only; runtime capture and hardware/media-path behavior are out of scope.
